data.title: Rice University named Official Houston World Cup 2026 Host City Supporter, key player in event planning.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, Rice University was designated as an Official Houston World Cup 2026 Host City Supporter. This recognition positions the state's prominent higher education institution at the forefront of arrangements for the upcoming global sporting extravaganza. The designation highlights Rice University's role in promoting the prestigious event and aligns with its commitment to...

data.title: SanDisk (SNDK) delivers strong Q2 report, with stock moving; Cantor Fitzgerald raises price target to $800.
data.description: SanDisk Corp. (NASDAQ: SNDK) recently released a remarkable second-quarter report along with promising future guidance. Analysts are expressing particularly optimistic views, resulting in a surge in SNDK stock. Among the positive reactions, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse raised the price target from $550 to $800, maintaining an Overweight rating on the stock. Muse emphasized key market drivers such as a 64% increase in Data Center demand due to AI applications, prompting customers to plan for capacity needs up to 2030. Additionally, the scarcity of NAND is posing a significant challenge to data center expansions, positioning SanDisk favorably in the market. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Alliance Cigar appoints Zachary Medwin as Vice President of Sales, to lead national sales strategy.
data.description: In Drum, Pennsylvania, on January 30, 2026, Alliance Cigar, a prominent distributor of premium cigars, disclosed the selection of Zachary Medwin as the Vice President of Sales. In his new position, Medwin will be responsible for directing the national sales strategy of Alliance Cigars and supervising crucial sales initiatives.

data.title: CEOs of Coinbase and JPMorgan disagreed over market structure bill at Davos. Armstrong withdrew support from US crypto bill after clashing with Dimon.
data.description: Before and after revealing that Coinbase would no longer support a significant US cryptocurrency bill, Brian Armstrong engaged with the media and participated in discussions at the Davos event, reportedly engaging in discussions with Jamie Dimon.

data.title: Prosperity Bancshares will acquire Stellar Bancorp for $2.002 billion, creating the second largest Texas bank.
data.description: Prosperity Bancshares, Inc. (PB) and Stellar Bancorp, Inc. (STEL) have seen their stock prices move in different directions following the announcement of their respective earnings reports and a merger agreement. Under the deal, Prosperity will exchange 0.3803 of its common stock shares along with $11.36 in cash for each share of Stellar common stock. The agreement values the transaction at around $2.002 billion, calculated based on Prosperity's closing price of $72.90 on January 27, 2026. Both companies' Boards of Directors have unanimously approved the merger, which is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending regulatory clearances and standard closing conditions. David Zalman, the Senior Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of... (For the complete article, refer to Benzinga.com)

data.title: Natural gas up 30%, silver up 13% to $116 amidst market movements, Henry Hub contract trades above $6, gold rose 2.2%, dollar weak.
data.description: U.S. natural gas prices surged more than 30% due to Winter Storm Fern, causing the largest weekly percentage gain for Nymex front-month contracts. Henry Hub front-month contract surpassed $6 per million British thermal units from $3 a week ago, marking a 125% increase. Gold rose 2.2% to $5,100, while silver surged 13% to $116 per ounce, with a rolling 12-month gain of 266%, the highest since 1980. The dollar weakened and the Japanese yen strengthened; there were speculations of currency intervention involving the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. U.S. equities also saw an upward trend. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Gold hits record high at $5,300/oz, up 22% this month. Silver at $114/oz, surging 59% year-to-date. Memory stocks soar after Seagate's guidance raise. Wall Street sees modest gains. Fed likely to keep interest rates unchanged at 3.50%–3.75%.
data.description: On Wednesday, Wall Street saw moderate gains with gold reaching a record high above $5,300 an ounce before the Federal Open Market Committee meeting. Gold has surged over 22% this month, its best rise since January 1980. Silver also climbed nearly 3% to $114 per ounce, aiming for its highest month on record after a 59% rise year-to-date. US equities started strong, with the S&P 500 touching 7,000 briefly before dropping 30 points during midday trading. The Nasdaq 100 rose 0.4%, surpassing 26,000 towards the October record of 26,182. Chipmakers like Seagate Technology Inc. beat expectations, raising full-year guidance due to global supply issues and high AI demand, sending its shares up 20%. Rivals Sandisk Corp. and Western Digital Corp. rose 7.3% and 9% respectively, whereas Micron Technology Inc. gained over 5%. The Federal Reserve is expected to maintain interest rates at 3.50%–3.75%, hinting at no urgency to adjust borrowing costs. Chair Jerome Powell's upcoming press conference is under scrutiny following a recent criminal investigation into the Fed's building renovation project.

data.title: Fed keeps federal funds rate at 3.5%–3.75% after 3 cuts, stating solid economic pace but elevated inflation.
data.description: The Federal Reserve decided to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.5%–3.75% in their January 2026 statement, halting the easing trend following three rate decreases last year, which dropped borrowing expenses to their lowest level since 2022. The Federal Open Market Committee upgraded its characterization of economic growth to a "solid pace," a change from the previous description of "moderate pace." The statement noted that the unemployment rate has exhibited signs of stabilization, a shift from earlier indications that it had slightly increased. The Federal Reserve acknowledged that inflation levels are still "somewhat elevated." Fed Chair Jerome Powell was also mentioned in the news release.
Week Ahead: Economic Indicators 2nd – 6th February (US)
